results.title: Carcase constructions
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Basic woodwork”.
Using a knife box as an example, this film describes how boards can be joined at the corners. No other credits available.

results.title: Basic woodwork: Preparing the wood
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Basic woodwork”.
After explaining the need for making a rough sketch and dimensional drawings of the article to be made, this film goes on to explain the drawing up of a cutting list of the sizes of the various pieces of wood required for the job.

results.title: Television system
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Basic Television”
Explains the principles of television broadcasting, from the camera tube at the studio to the receiver. Shows how the image is brokenm down into picture elements, clarifies the procedure of interfacing; explains the function of each component of the video signal, shows how the scanning raster is produced and the image reproduced in shades of black and white on the screen.

results.title: Television receivers
results.creator_credit: Loucks and Norling Studios and McGraw-Hill Text-Films
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Basic Television”
Diagrams and live action are used to identify the sections of the receiver circuit and to summarise the functions of the receiver. Explains the specific functions of each section in a typical intercarrier sound receiver circuit, from the antenna and transmission line to the picture tube.

results.title: Practical TV alignment
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Basic Television”.
Explains how to use television alignment equipment to obtain the visual response curve of a receiver on the oscilloscope screen. Practical demonstrations are used to show when a receiver is in need of alignment and to show the use of various types of equipment in testing alignment. The series is designed for screening to students studying television principles and servicing and is correlated with the McGraw text-book “Basic Television” by Bernard Croft.

results.title: Localising troubles
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Basic Television”.
An intercarrier sound receiver using the flyback high voltage supply is used as a typical example in explaining how to identify trouble symptoms and localise them in a specific section of the receiver by studying the three indicators present in the receiver itself - raster, picture and sound.

results.title: Deflection output circuits
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Basic Television”.
An explanation of vertical and horizontal output circuits for magnetic deflection, staring with the vertical output stages as a typical deflections amplifier. The first part pf the film shows how the sawtooth scanning current in the deflection coils corresponds to linear scanning of the electron beam; it explains oscillations and damping in the output circuit. The remainder of the film builds up the complete horizontal out-put circuit, step by step.

results.title: Antenna installation
results.creator_credit: Loucks and Norling Studios and McGraw-Hill Films
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Basic Television”.
Part of a series of films designed for screening to students studying television principles and servicing. This episode uses animation and live action to illustrate directivity and the characteristics of the basic dipole antenna, and to show a typical step-by-step installation of an antenna. It includes demonstrations to illustrate a chimney strap mounting, the use of stand-offs, the use of the Rawl drill, and the installation of the lightning arrester and ground rod. NB: This film may be considered out-of-date but useful for basic instructions (11/97).
